item.title: Update your ABC News homepage for more local stories
item.description: We've got an ABC News homepage for you. Adjust your settings for the right mix of local and national news.

item.title: Call for button battery redesign after new study reveals horrific injuries and fatalities
item.description: A new study reveals the horrific injuries suffered after swallowing or inserting button batteries but manufacturers aren't prioritising their redesign.

item.title: The US election is about style more than substance. It's why Kamala Harris needs to nail her DNC speech in about eight seconds
item.description: When the Democrat presidential candidate Kamala Harris delivers her speech to the Democratic National Convention today, style will be almost everything.

item.title: Have Your Say: What was the issue, policy or event that decided your vote?
item.description: After a five-week campaign, the results are in with Labor returned to government. Is there a policy or issue that swayed your vote? Have Your Say.

item.title: Voters tell ABC's Your Say they want politicians with a vision, not bickering
item.description: Voters tell ABC's Your Say campaign they want courage and vision from politicians, not bickering.

item.title: Australians tell ABC's Your Say they are struggling more than ever with housing costs
item.description: Voters tell ABC's Your Say campaign they are struggling more than ever with housing costs.

item.title: Graduates in 'debt vortex' as HECS front and centre for first time in an election
item.description: New research shows a sharp rise in HECS debts, despite graduate earning power declining, as student loans become a potent election issue for the first time.

item.title: Housing advocates seek federal funding election promise for co-op model
item.description: Advocates admit co-operative housing models are not for everyone but say for the right residents, they offer more security and affordable pricing than traditional rentals.

item.title: Next-generation voters call for better engagement ahead of federal election
item.description: Young people in Albury say issues that directly affect them and their communities are not being addressed by major parties for the 2025 election campaign.

item.title: Melbourne seat of Calwell highlights Labor woes as Gaza pain galvanises Muslim voters
item.description: Dissatisfaction with the Albanese government's stance on Israel-Palestine among many Arab and Muslim voters threatens to unseat Labor MPs, in some places for the first time.

item.title: Cost of living, housing among key concerns in SA marginal seat of Boothby
item.description: At this year’s federal election, the South Australian seat of Boothby is one of the state’s two marginal seats. As part of the ABC’s “Your Say” campaign, we spoke to residents in the electorate about their biggest concerns.

item.title: Energy projects in South West WA threaten votes for major federal parties
item.description: Australia's two major parties have tied their federal election fates to controversial energy projects in WA's South West where the issues have polarised voters.

item.title: Tiwi Islanders eager to have their say as remote voting for the federal election begins
item.description: Voters in the small remote community of Pirlangimpi on the Tiwi Islands, north of Darwin, have become some of the first to cast their votes for the federal election.

item.title: Mobile coverage is an election issue in regional areas. What's been promised?
item.description: For many regional and remote Australians, reliable mobile coverage can be life-saving. They are desperate for both sides of politics to take meaningful action towards eliminating black spots.

item.title: Health, climate and community sport: What older voters in the Tasmanian seat of Bass are talking about this election
item.description: The federal seat of Bass is held by a margin of just 1.4 per cent. For these older voters who live in the electorate, health, climate and community sport are the issues that will decide their vote.

item.title: Pauline Hanson says she has growing support in Canberra. But does it pass the pub test?
item.description: The conventional wisdom is that Canberra is a largely left-leaning city, but the national capital is now among places in One Nation's sights as support for the party surges across the country.

item.title: Lodging too early and other mistakes people make when doing their taxes
item.description: With side hustles, working from home, crypto investments, renting out rooms and receiving income through digital platforms or apps, experts say people are at greater risk of making errors with their tax.

item.title: World Cup: Lifeless Germany fall as Paraguay show tenacity to die another day
item.description: Paraguay's upset penalty-shootout win over Germany at the World Cup, while stunning, was not entirely unexpected, as they showed impressive determination to die another day.

item.title: Don't ask me what's culturally appropriate, I don't know either
item.description: After sending out invitations to a belated elopement party, I was suddenly fielding calls from friends who wanted to wear saris but were worried that it might be disrespectful.

item.title: Fossil kept in drawer for 40 years is Antarctica’s first dinosaur bone
item.description: A bone found in Antarctica in 1985, and kept in a drawer in England for almost four decades, turns out to belong to one of the largest types of dinosaurs to ever walk the Earth.

item.title: NT residents are the nation's happiest, university study finds
item.description: Northern Territory residents are the happiest in the nation, a new university study has found, with access to nature, warm weather and cultural diversity among the theories for Territorians' sunny attitudes.

item.title: How to protect your child from head knocks in sport
item.description: When your child decides they are keen on playing sport, there are lots of factors to consider as a parent, and after recent news stories about chronic traumatic encephalopathy, keeping them safe from head injuries may be front of mind.

item.title: Photos reveal room where Australian man allegedly murdered Thai teenager
item.description: New photographs reveal the cluttered room where 17-year-old Tunchanok Donhomla was allegedly murdered by Australian man Simon Peter Carman.

item.title: From talking sheep to incel horror, here are the best movies of 2026 so far
item.description: It's officially halfway through 2026 and the year so far has brought cinematic delights as well as a fair share of silver-screen stinkers. Here are our critics' top picks.

item.title: Conversion practice ban legislation set to be debated in Tasmanian parliament
item.description: Tasmania was the last Australian jurisdiction to decriminalise homosexuality, but since then it has gone on to pass some of the country's most inclusive legislation. There's one final frontier — conversion practices.

item.title: Maya Joint says walking with Serena Williams onto Wimbledon's centre court her 'craziest moment'
item.description: Australian Maya Joint had to catch a glimpse of Serena Williams in the days leading up to their Wimbledon opener just to remove some of the mythos around the 23-time grand slam champion.

item.title: World Cup: 'No tomorrow' as Socceroos brace for date with destiny
item.description: The Socceroos' World Cup adventure will either end or become historic when they face Egypt on Friday.

item.title: Is anyone happy with Australia's housing market? A new film asks the question
item.description: If you've been wondering how and why Australian house prices spun out of control you're not alone. One filmmaker was so depressed by it he went out and shot a documentary.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: Mexico madness at the Azteca, big Mo Salah injury update
item.description: Mexico fans enjoy a night to remember at the Azteca, Egypt gives a big update on Mo Salah's fitness, and Ronald Koeman quits as the Netherlands coach after a shootout disaster. Here are today's World Cup quick hits.

item.title: ARN Media set to dump Karl Stefanovic from radio show, presenter defends controversial interview
item.description: Karl Stefanovic will be dumped from his new radio show amid fallout from his interview with Tommy Robinson, the ABC understands.

item.title: Village People founding member, lead singer Victor Willis dead at 74
item.description: The lead singer and a founding member of the Village People disco group has died at the age of 74.

item.title: USA's World Cup dream alive despite red card against Bosnia and Herzegovina
item.description: The US seals progression to the last 16 with a 2-0 win over Bosnia and Herzegovina despite losing a man to a red card, while Belgium converts a penalty in extra-time to beat Senegal 3-2.

item.title: Matthew Flinders's steamy letters reveal a hidden side to the man who circumnavigated Australia
item.description: Flinders circumnavigated and named Australia. Impressive stuff, but he also wrote many impassioned letters and lived a life full of crushes.

item.title: Why this Vanuatu village worships a shape-shifting spirit called John Frum
item.description: On Vanuatu's Tanna Island, I immerse myself in a culture that worships a shape-shifting spirit known as John Frum.

item.title: Albanese and Chalmers see reform and risk-taking as an antidote to populism
item.description: The slight recovery in Labor's polling shifts assumptions about what's in store for the remainder of this parliamentary term.

item.title: Heading the ball is integral to football but concerns about its risks are growing
item.description: With all eyes on the World Cup, Australian football's management of the risks associated with the practice of heading the ball, such as dementia and CTE, is in the spotlight.

item.title: Up to 200 XPeng electric-vehicle owners still chasing $5,000 cashback promised months ago
item.description: When Mustansir Milky took the leap and bought a new electric vehicle, he didn't expect to have to fight to get the $5,000 cashback payment he was promised.

item.title: Tunchanok Donhomla's family lays Thai teen's ashes to rest and demands death penalty for alleged murder
item.description: The family of a Thai teenager allegedly killed by an Australian man has placed her cremated ashes to rest at a local Buddhist temple as they seek justice for her murder.

item.title: Satellite images reveal extent of Venezuela's earthquake devastation
item.description: Venezuela's devastating earthquakes flattened buildings and coated parts of the northern coast in dust and debris, satellite imagery shows.

item.title: Redress scheme will not clear backlog before closure at current processing rate
item.description: At its current processing rate, the scheme will not be able to clear its backlog before it closes.

item.title: Pornhub blocks advertising from Australian AI porn site OurDream
item.description: Australian pornography website OurDream has had its advertising suspended by Pornhub after the ABC revealed OurDream's content could include child exploitation material.

item.title: World Cup hero Harry Kane leads England to final 16 with 2-1 victory over DR Congo
item.description: Facing World Cup elimination at the hands of DR Congo, England needed a hero to step up when it mattered most. Harry Kane was that man.

item.title: Russian daredevils climb Empire State Building to unfurl 'love' banner above New York
item.description: Two Russian daredevils, Angela Nikolau and Vanya Beerkus, claim to be the duo who scaled the 102-storey, 443-metre tower for an apparent show of love and a marriage proposal.

item.title: US Navy helicopter goes down in Arabian Sea sparking search for missing crew member
item.description: A US Navy service member is missing and three others are injured after their MH-60S Sea Hawk helicopter went down in the Arabian Sea.

item.title: NT celebrates 2026 cracker night with a bang as fireworks light up the skies
item.description: People across the Northern Territory have spent the night setting off their own fireworks for the Territory's annual cracker night celebrations, lighting up the sky with colour and keeping firefighters and paramedics busy.

item.title: Namadgi National Park shows signs of climate change as rangers intervene
item.description: Like melting glaciers in Antarctica or bleached coral on the Great Barrier Reef, Namadgi National Park is showing signs of damage linked to global warming.

item.title: When do the Socceroos play next? How to watch Australia vs Egypt at the 2026 World Cup
item.description: Australia's journey at the 2026 World Cup continues this weekend as the Socceroos take on Egypt. Here's everything you need to know.

item.title: Car catches fire and fireworks explode out of boot
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Financial misconduct compensation scheme faces $170 million cost blowout
item.description: The First Guardian and Shield super disasters, and Dixon Advisory fallout, is causing a cost blowout in the taxpayer-funded scheme that is designed to compensate victims of financial misconduct.

item.title: Christian Brothers granted pause on child abuse victim payouts due to financial strain
item.description: The Christian Brothers religious order has been granted a moratorium on all civil claims against it, after arguing it was running out of money to pay victims of child sexual abuse.

item.title: Mile Jedinak knows what it's like to take a World Cup penalty and he has advice for the Socceroos
item.description: Now an assistant coach for the Socceroos, and with a penalty shootout a possibility when Australia face Egypt in the round of 32, Mile Jedinak knows what it takes to seize the moment.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: Feuding Belgians save the day, England's claims of 'disadvantage'
item.description: Belgium stars turn on each other before combining to save their team, England makes claims of "disadvantage", and DR Congo's goalkeeper gives it all he's got. Here are today's World Cup quick hits.

item.title: Micro 'cockroach' drones help search for Venezuela earthquake survivors
item.description: A week after devastating twin earthquakes struck Venezuela rescue teams are using drones, dogs, cameras and specialised listening equipment to search for survivors.

item.title: $160 million government loan aims to protect an outback fertiliser industry
item.description: Ryowa Group has received a loan for its outback Queensland Phosphate Hill asset as the federal and state governments move to ramp up domestic fertiliser production capability.

item.title: Are parents reconsidering letting kids play footy due to CTE risks?
item.description: How is the conversation about lasting brain disease from repeated head knocks on the sporting field impacting parents' decisions about what sports their children play?

item.title: Government urged to crack down on NDIS kickbacks and bad actors as fraud inquiry handed down
item.description: A Labor-led parliamentary inquiry into fraud in the NDIS has made 12 recommendations to improve the integrity of the scheme.

item.title: Former St Mary's Hostel in Alice Springs heritage listed as significant Stolen Generations site
item.description: Former residents say the heritage listing of the St Mary's Hostel site in Alice Springs is an important step towards healing, truth-telling and remembering the children taken there under assimilation-era policies.

item.title: NSW MP Helen Dalton's agricultural company fined over quad bike death
item.description: The court heard the 82-year-old worker was not wearing a helmet and the quad had no rollover protection when the crash occurred.

item.title: Oscar Piastri and Max Verstappen at centre of F1 driver change speculation
item.description: F1's rumour mill has been in overdrive in recent times about Oscar Piastri's future at McLaren, with his boss having to hose down suggestions the Australian will be driving anything other than a Papaya-coloured F1 car in 2027.

item.title: Russia strikes in central Kyiv kill at least 20 and injure dozens
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy cut his Dublin trip short as the strikes trapped paramedics and drivers at an ambulance station.

item.title: Look inside US President Donald Trump's new $580m Air Force One jet
item.description: The jet, gifted to the US government by Qatar, has been retrofitted, with Trump shaking off concerns over the cost.

item.title: Adelaide Thunderbirds fans will be in full cry at Super Netball Grand Final
item.description: Among the devoted Thunderbirds fans making the journey to Melbourne for the grand final against the Vixens are the Rawlins family, including sisters Tess and Eve.

item.title: Venezuelans make desperate pleas for help a week after devastating double earthquake
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Man bids $16k to take his budget tax grudge to the tennis court with Albanese
item.description: A mortgage broker forked out $16,500 on Canberra’s night of nights to take his grudge about budget tax directly up to the prime minister in a tennis match at the Lodge.

item.title: Former Sydney childcare worker David James jailed for up to 12 years over child abuse offences
item.description: A painstaking victim identification process was undertaken by Australian Federal Police to establish his offending occurred at six of out-of-school hours care centres over a three-year period.

item.title: Visa fees increase without warning, sparking outrage and disappointment
item.description: International students and families say they feel shocked and exploited after visa fees across dozens of categories rose on July 1 without warning, with some surging more than 200 per cent.

item.title: Senate orders Privacy Commissioner to release report on American Express security failures
item.description: The Privacy Commissioner has been ordered by parliament to hand over a secret report detailing its investigation into American Express's technology security failures, after using gag orders and legal threats to keep it confidential.

item.title: Teen accused of abducting 17yo girl near Melbourne school faces court
item.description: A teen has been accused of abducting a 17-year-old girl from near a Melbourne school with the intention of forcing her to marry him.

item.title: Gina Rinehart and estranged kids to pay large legal costs after royalties trial
item.description: Mining billionaire Gina Rinehart and her two estranged children will have to pay a large chunk of the legal costs able to be recovered from a mammoth trial that determined the payment of royalties from some of Australia's most lucrative iron ore mines, a WA judge has ruled.

item.title: Lions hold on for win over Geelong in Kardinia classic
item.description: Brisbane Lions earn a second straight win over Geelong at Kardinia Park in a high-scoring epic, as the Lions jump into the top four.

item.title: John Dewar accepts UOW appointment would benefit KordaMentha, ICAC hears
item.description: ICAC hears that John Dewar wanted his UOW appointment to benefit KordaMentha and his acknowledgement there could be a perception of a conflict of interest.

item.title: Eight Thai monks killed after boy drives truck into procession in Mukdahan province
item.description: An 11-year-old has driven his parents' truck into a Buddhist procession in Thailand, killing eight monks.

item.title: New South Wales government cracks down on sales of potentially deadly nitrous oxide
item.description: After a 7.30 investigation exposed the increasing dangers of nitrous oxide use, NSW has announced reforms will come into place shortly.

item.title: Liberal MP Simon Kennedy says anti-gambling changes contain streaming 'loophole'
item.description: Liberal MP Simon Kennedy argues the federal government's proposed changes to gambling advertising laws contain a streaming loophole.

item.title: De Minaur cruises through to Wimbledon third round with straight-sets win
item.description: Australian Alex de Minaur has raced into the third round at Wimbledon with a straight-sets victory over tricky French veteran Adrian Mannarino.

item.title: UK PM formally apologises over forced adoptions, calling the practice a 'stain on our history'
item.description: UK PM Keir Starmer has formally apologised for the British state's role in separating tens of thousands of unmarried mothers from their babies.

item.title: Security guard rescued from basement eight days after Venezuela earthquakes
item.description: A 43 year old man has been pulled from the rubble of a collapsed shopping centre after being trapped for eight days after earthquakes in Venezuela.
